bonus_fact: Angola is home to unique wildlife, including giant sable antelopes.

title: Alien Invaders Take Over TV
story: Pointy-headed aliens invaded living rooms across America in 1977. On January 15, the Coneheads made their hilarious debut on Saturday Night Live. These oddball characters from the planet Remulak quickly became fan favorites with their strange speech and wacky misunderstandings of Earth culture.
activity_question: What planet were the Coneheads from?

title: Cowboys Lasso Victory in Super Bowl XII
story: Touchdown! The Dallas Cowboys galloped to victory in Super Bowl XII, held at the Louisiana Superdome in 1978. On January 15, they roped in a 27-10 win against the Denver Broncos. It was a historic game, as two Cowboys players, Harvey Martin and Randy White, shared the Most Valuable Player award for their stellar defensive play.
activity_question: Which team won Super Bowl XII?

title: Telethon Raises Millions for Cerebral Palsy
story: Phones rang off the hook as generous donors opened their hearts and wallets. On January 15, 1989, a special telethon for Cerebral Palsy brought in an incredible $22.6 million! People from all walks of life pitched in to support those living with this condition. The event showed how communities can come together to make a real difference in others' lives.

To launch a new TV show

title: A Royal Quest for New France
story: King Francis I had big dreams for France. In 1541, on January 15, he sent explorer Jean-Francois Roberval on an exciting mission. The king wanted Roberval to build a new French colony across the ocean. This land would be called New France, and it was the start of Frances adventure in North America.

title: Baseball Boosts Morale in Wartime
story: As World War II raged, President Franklin D. Roosevelt made a surprising play. On January 15, 1942, he sent the 'Green Light Letter' to baseball's top official. This special message encouraged America's favorite pastime to keep going strong. Roosevelt believed baseball could lift spirits and give people a much-needed break from worrying about the war.
